titlestmt.titleproper: Register to the Miles Kastendieck Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Bogdan Dumitriu and Richard Boursy

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10601196.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 00:10

did.abstract: Scrapbooks of articles by the music critic Miles Kastendieck, as well as records of the Music Critics' Circle of New York City, and miscellaneous other materials.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Miles Kastendieck Papers were established in the Gilmore Music Library by Miles and Clementine Kastendieck in 1995.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Miles Kastendieck Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 117, The Miles Kastendieck Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Miles Merwin Kastendieck (1905-2001) was a music critic and educator. A graduate of Yale (B.A. '27, Mus.B. '28, Ph.D. '32), he wrote for the Brooklyn Eagle, New York Journal American, New York World Journal Tribune, and Christian Science Monitor. He taught English at Poly Prep Country Day School in Brooklyn, and wrote a book about the school's history, as well as a book about Thomas Campion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes Kastendieck's newspaper articles (mostly in scrapbooks), various other items by and about Kastendieck, and records of the Music Critics' Circle of New York City.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into 4 series: I. Scrapbooks. II. Miscellaneous. III. Dissertation-Related Materials. IV. Clippings.

corpname.part: Music Critics' Circle of New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of scrapbooks containing reviews and other articles by Miles Kastendieck. For conservation reasons, the scrapbooks have been microfilmed, and researchers should consult the microfilm reels rather than the originals.
Kastendieck prepared indexes for Scrapbooks 2 and 3. These indexes are available in Series II.
Readers interested in Kastendieck's work as a music critic should also consult Series IV, Clippings.

odd.head: General
odd.p: There is an index for this scrapbook in Box 5, Folder 7.

odd.head: General
odd.p: There is an index for this scrapbook in Box 5, Folder 7.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains one letter each from Edwin Baily and Harold Burck, as well as a card from Kastendieck's students.

did.unittitle: Drafts of program notes or lectures

odd.head: General
odd.p: No index is available for the other scrapbooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Used by Kastendieck for Christmas cards and announcements.

did.unittitle: Miles Kastendieck's files on Roger Sessions
Music Critics Circle of New York. Records
Music Critics Circle of New York. Records
Music Critics Circle of New York. Records
Music Critics Circle of New York. Records
Picture of, and biographical information on, Miles Kastendieck

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Term paper (1930), on the poet and musician who became the subject of Kastendieck's dissertation (1932).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This correspondence concerns Kastendieck's efforts to publish his dissertation. It was eventually published by Oxford University Press in 1938 under the title England's Musical Poet: Thomas Campion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of loose clippings, chiefly from Kastendieck's work as a music critc. Most of them are from 1967-1973, a period not covered by the scrapbooks in Series I, which cover another phase of Kastendieck's career.
